title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Model Lens: Numpy Arrays
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int numpy arrays increment in a tested numerical Python notebook and module. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.
Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
